scope: Senegal tactical and coaching team_profile packet only

source_log.source: FSF official World Cup provisional squad article
source_log.note: Published 2026-05-21. Confirms Pape Thiaw, provisional 28-man World Cup list, and pre-tournament friendlies vs USA and Saudi Arabia.

source_log.source: FSF official World Cup retained players article
source_log.note: Published 2026-06-02. Official final-retained World Cup article exists, but the accessible text in this page is limited and appears image-based.
Accessible search snippet confirms the current Senegal squad page and core player names.
Sudan 0-0 Senegal, 2025-03-23. FSF says Senegal largely dominated but failed to convert chances.
Saudi Arabia 0-0 Senegal, 2026-06-10. Final World Cup warm-up ended in a scoreless draw.
Lists USA 3-2 Senegal friendly and the 2026 retained squad article.
Describes Pape Thiaw as attack-minded while balancing flair and discipline.
Published 2026-01-21. States Senegal returned home after winning AFCON 2025.

notes: File created first by main thread and then filled incrementally.
No match score prediction, group projection, champion prediction, or betting advice.
This packet only covers Senegal team_profile.
Current version uses official FSF, FIFA, and CAF public sources because the local team packet is still a skeleton.
fan_summary_en: Senegal are not only about power and speed; they can slow a match into a physical grind and then suddenly break it open with Mane, Ismaila Sarr, and Nicolas Jackson attacking depth.

notes: Primary sample is drawn from 2025 World Cup qualifying, AFCON 2025 cycle information, and 2026 World Cup warm-up matches.
The local structured fact packet is still incomplete, so some role and in-game adjustment claims remain provisional.

coach_profile.summary: Pape Thiaw has kept Senegal's strong physical and transition-heavy base while adding a more attack-minded release of pace and wide runners. Public CAF material describes him as attack-minded but disciplined, which broadly matches the available sample.

notes: FSF published a provisional 28-player World Cup list on 2026-05-21 and a retained-players article on 2026-06-02.
Because the retained article is not fully accessible as clean text, this packet relies on the FSF provisional list plus the FIFA current squad page for core-player confirmation.
Core tactical analysis focuses on repeatedly referenced leaders: Sadio Mane, Kalidou Koulibaly, Edouard Mendy, Ismaila Sarr, Nicolas Jackson, Pape Matar Sarr, and Idrissa Gana Gueye.

team_shape.in_possession_shape: Often a back four base with one fullback advancing, wide forwards attacking depth, and a midfield triangle balancing second balls and central coverage.
team_shape.out_of_possession_shape: Usually a compact 4-1-4-1 or 4-5-1 look, with the wingers recovering and the midfield screening central access.
role_asymmetry: Left side often carries more star gravity through Mane.
Right side often becomes the direct running lane for Ismaila Sarr or another pace outlet.
The midfield three are chosen to preserve duel strength and transition coverage before pure chance-creation volume.

key_roles.note: Still the main reference for elite attacking moments, big-game responsibility, and left-side threat. His value is not only scoring but also forcing defensive attention and changing match rhythm.
Main center-back organizer, duel leader, and defensive-line authority. His presence strongly shapes Senegal's defensive floor.
Important for box security, high-ball handling, and preserving low-event match control.
Key outlet for pace, wide transition attacks, and stretching the line vertically.
Offers channel running, carry threat, and space attack rather than only fixed-box finishing.
Brings running power, pressure resistance, and forward momentum from midfield.
Still important for ball-winning, central protection, and match management in physically demanding games.

attack.summary: Senegal's attack is most dangerous when the game opens. They can attack through pace, wide runs, and direct depth early, then use physical presence and second balls to sustain pressure.
notes: Mane and Ismaila Sarr are the main wide-release threats.
Nicolas Jackson adds central mobility and channel running rather than purely static penalty-box presence.
In settled attacks, Senegal can look less fluid than top possession-heavy teams and may rely on individual acceleration or crosses more than elaborate central combinations.

defense.summary: Senegal's defensive identity is built on duel strength, center-back authority, and a willingness to alternate between pressing and compact recovery depending on the opponent.
notes: They do not need to press at maximum height all match to stay competitive.
The core protection comes from Koulibaly plus experienced midfield screening.
The main structural concern is the space behind advancing fullbacks or wide defenders when Senegal commit runners forward and the first counterpressure fails.

set_pieces.attack: Set-piece threat is supported by athleticism, center-back size, and second-ball aggression.
set_pieces.defense: Set-piece defense is generally solid when the main aerial leaders are available, but the full taker-target map remains incomplete.

coach_tendencies.leading_adjustments: When ahead, Senegal tend to protect central zones first and keep at least one fast outlet live for counters rather than fully abandoning transition threat.
coach_tendencies.trailing_adjustments: When trailing, the likely pathway is more direct width, added pace, and a faster tempo rather than an extreme possession-based structural rewrite.
coach_tendencies.summary: Thiaw appears willing to attack, but the available sample still points to a tournament logic built on defensive respectability first and pace release second.

attack_ceiling.note: Top-end pace and wide threats can create explosive attacking spells, especially in open matches.

defensive_floor.note: With Koulibaly, Mendy, and experienced midfield shielding, Senegal's defensive floor is strong.

set_piece_attack.note: Athleticism and aerial capacity make set pieces a meaningful secondary weapon.

tempo_preference.note: Senegal can slow the game into a physical battle or accelerate sharply when transition lanes open.

transition_attack.note: This is one of Senegal's clearest high-value attacking phases.

transition_defense_risk.note: Wide recovery and rest-defense spacing can be stressed when advanced runners and fullbacks are caught ahead of the ball.

key_player_dependency.note: Mane remains the main gravity piece in attack, while Koulibaly and Mendy anchor the defensive floor.

market_relevant_flags.note: His ability to accelerate attacks and demand extra defensive attention changes Senegal's attacking ceiling.
A more mobile striker profile can make Senegal more dangerous in channels and early transition attacks.

market_relevant_flags.factor: Gana Gueye and midfield shield availability
market_relevant_flags.note: Midfield screen quality strongly affects Senegal's defensive control and second-ball recovery.
The more aggressively Senegal push wide defenders, the more both attacking width and transition exposure rise.

verification_questions.claim: Senegal's stable base shape under Pape Thiaw is a 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 four-back structure.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: This should be separated by opponent level and by whether the frontline includes Jackson, Mane, and Ismaila Sarr together.

verification_questions.claim: Senegal's attack is strongest in transition and depth attacks rather than in long spells of complex settled possession.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Needs broader event-level evidence across different match states and opponent profiles.

verification_questions.claim: Koulibaly is the single most important defensive-floor player in the squad.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Should be tested against matches where Mendy or the midfield screen had to compensate for center-back absences or decline.

verification_questions.claim: Wide defensive exposure behind advanced fullbacks is one of Senegal's main structural risks.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Requires video-backed review to prove it is a repeated team issue rather than isolated game state noise.

verification_questions.claim: Pape Thiaw remains tournament-pragmatic even if his public profile is more attack-minded than some earlier Senegal setups.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Needs more complete substitution and late-game management evidence across stronger opponents.

verification_questions.claim: The final retained World Cup squad still centers on the same veteran spine plus a pace-heavy wide attack group.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: The final official retained list is not yet fully text-verified in this packet.

data.completion_note: First Senegal team_profile is complete enough for a tactical handoff but remains partial because the local data packet is still skeletal and the final official retained list is not fully text-verified in this file.
